mechanism_narrative: Lower absolute levels of the federal funds rate compress the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in <55% of low-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and triggers recession risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent over a 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth across durable and non-durable sectors, reinforcing corporate revenue expectations and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.96 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and rising input costs that compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The negative predictive power of declining INDPRO vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) when FEDFUNDS is rising, or returns are positive in >65% of tightening-cycle drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening labor market conditions that boost household income and consumer confidence, directly supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4 percent signal accelerating price pressures that force central banks to tighten policy aggressively, raising discount rates and triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4 percent, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates a decoupling of output and labor demand, signaling an impending recessionary downturn that suppresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining GDPC1 and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands while the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0 percent, it demonstrates resilient supply-side capacity that sustains earnings growth despite higher borrowing costs, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during high-rate periods fails to generate positive 5-day returns, with the signal reversing direction or losing significance (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward tests.
